item.title: Europe must have the financial power to match its economic heft
item.description: The continent’s savings should be used to invest in its own future, not someone else’s

item.title: Maybe the trusty conglomerate playbook is due a rewrite
item.description: Perhaps it’s managers, not markets, that are inefficient

item.title: The great AI data centre cover-up
item.description: Tech companies need to come clean about the mounting environmental fallout of their race to build more hubs

item.title: The unsustainable upward trajectory of UK debt
item.description: Over time, if there is no tax-and-spend adjustment, the changes needed to public finances will be painful

item.title: The male wellness guide to peptides
item.description: Injectable amino acids are being used for muscle gain, sexual health and skin tanning — but the benefits are questionable

item.title: The BoE vs AI exuberance
item.description: AI bonds are the new gilts

item.title: The battle for the future of democratic republics
item.description: Celebrations in the US for its 250th anniversary are in part a wake

item.title: Ocado’s troubles show the ‘founder trap’ in miniature
item.description: While Tim Steiner may have lost some credibility, it’s hard to imagine a professional manager turning the business around

item.title: What if your drawdown strategy was ‘eat what you kill’?
item.description: Forget the 4 Per Cent Rule, according to the Canasta Strategy, you should let the market decide your pension spending

item.title: Lessons from a shipwreck
item.description: The sinking of the Vasa has been analysed by management consultants — with mixed results

item.title: Streamers ignore omnivorous consumers at their peril
item.description: Sony’s plan to drop physical discs endangers the company and its customers

item.title: China needs another Zhu Rongji to cut industrial excess
item.description: Manufacturing glut has revived memories of sweeping reforms under a charismatic reformist premier

item.title: Does moving to England affect our US prenup?
item.description: My husband and I were married in the US but have recently moved to London — does our agreement still hold?

item.title: Why retail investors matter more than ever to investment trusts
item.description: Boards must realise that the rise of smaller investors is ultimately a good thing

item.title: Electric trucks are coming to a highway near you
item.description: CATL has teamed up with Octopus to build 30 battery-swapping stations by 2035

item.title: Le Pen’s massive double gamble on presidency
item.description: Strategy might play well with the far-right leader’s core voters, but the French as a whole don’t buy it

item.title: How AI is changing the world of retail investment
item.description: Excessive regulation could hamper innovation and harm savers

item.title: Hertz gets a helping hand from an unlikely source: short sellers
item.description: Convertible bonds should keep rental car company out of immediate trouble

item.title: Farage struggles to be ringmaster of his own circus
item.description: In forcing a by-election, the Reform UK leader’s bold move looks to be backfiring

item.title: Trump and the Fifa debacle
item.description: The US president is the first leader since Benito Mussolini in 1934 to intervene publicly in his team’s favour

item.title: Advantage, Wall Street? Why finance firms seek sports stars for advice
item.description: Novak Djokovic is the latest of many athletic recruits to financial services

item.title: K-pop, markets edition
item.description: On Kospi index concentration

item.title: Labour and the Tories need to outflank the extremists
item.description: Kemi Badenoch and Andy Burnham must remember that elections are not fought and won on issues of culture alone

item.title: How to stop AI becoming the enemy of younger workers
item.description: ‘Seniority-biased’ hiring patterns in South Korea carry a lesson for the rest of the world

item.title: Roadside rescuers may still bump up against a driverless future
item.description: Self-driving cabs crash less, which is bad news for the AA’s and RAC’s accident assistance businesses
